Contact Rare & Manuscript Collections

Bayard Taylor papers, 1837-1878.
Collection Number: 14-18-1169

Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Cornell University Library

DESCRIPTIVE SUMMARY

Title:
Bayard Taylor papers, 1837-1878.
Repository:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Collection Number:
14-18-1169
Abstract:
Correspondence, journals, notebooks, scrapbooks, manuscripts, diaries, clippings, articles, sketches, and autobiographical notes pertaining to Taylor's life as a writer and traveler in North Africa, Scandinavia, Europe, and the Middle East.
Creator:
Taylor, Bayard, 1825-1878.
Quanitities:
9.2 cubic feet.
Language:
Collection material in English
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE

Traveler, translator, writer, man of letters, non-resident professor of German literature at Cornell University.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION

Correspondence, journals, notebooks, scrapbooks, manuscripts, diaries, clippings, articles, sketches, and autobiographical notes pertaining to Taylor's life as a writer and traveler in North Africa, Scandinavia, Europe, and the Middle East.
Also, a watercolor landscape by Taylor acquired by Dr. Francis Harvey Green and given to William F. Rochow in 1950, with "Gems of Thought," a card with excerpts from Taylor's writings selected by Green.
INFORMATION FOR USERS
Cite As:

Bayard Taylor papers, #14-18-1169. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
SUBJECTS

Subjects:
Authors, American
Europe -- Description and travel.
Africa -- Description and travel.
German literature -- Study and teaching.
Form and Genre Terms:
Watercolors.
Diaries
CONTAINER LIST
Container
Description
Date
Link to a partial finding aid. 4 MB PDF file.
Scope and Contents
Not all the material in the collection is covered by this guide.
Series I. Documents and manuscripts
Scope and Contents
Loose materials in envelopes--titles mainly taken from envelopes. BT stands for Bayard Taylor. Includes some correspondence.
Box 7 Folder 7
BT's connection with the centennial
 1876-07-04
Scope and Contents
Contains manuscripts of a hymn written by BT for the centennial and sent to Whittier. Correspondence from Jos. R. Hawley, President of the International Exhibition, US Centennial Commission
Box 7 Folder 10
Golden wedding masque and additional letters mentioned in Mrs. Taylors letter of June 14, 1905
 1905-06
Scope and Contents
Also contains printed catalogue of autograph leeters - a selection from the literary correspondence of BT and many others (June 21, 1905). Nothing appears to be related the a wedding, ets.
Box 7 Folder 13
Special letters and notes from the Taylor correspondence
 Date unknown
Box 7 Folder 14
Letters to Bayard Taylor
Scope and Contents
Names unidentified
Box 7 Folder 15
Document fragments, chiefly from BT
 Undated
Scope and Contents
Some fragments are imperfect. Also includes one undated note of Aldrich to Taylor.
Box 7 Folder 16
Notes, letters, and invitations to the Taylors for various functions
 Undated, date unknown, 1850-05-03
Scope and Contents
Folder labelled "curiosities." Invitations are in French. Also includes phrenological character of Bayard Taylor by Lorenzo Niles Fowler, May 3, 1850.
Box 7 Folder 17
Letters and memos about Faust
 Date unknown
Box 7 Folder 18
Faustiana letters and notes
 Date unknown
Box 7 Folder 19
Collections for Goethe in general
 Date unknown
Box 7 Folder 20
Official documents, St. Petersburg Mission
 1862-1863
Scope and Contents
Letters from W.H. Seward, Secretary of State, and copies of dispatches of B.T. from St. Petersburg
Box 7 Folder 23
Only Once
 1862
Scope and Contents
Original papers by various contributors, published for benefit of New York Infirmary for Women and Children.
Box 7 Folder 24
A Memorial of Fitz-Greene Halleck
 1877
Box 7 Folder 25
Translations from Bayard Taylor's English works into German
 Date unknown
Box 7 Folder 26
Dedication of the Halleck Monument at Guildford, Connecticut
 1869-07-08
Box 7 Folder 27
Clippings, etc.
 Date unknown
Box 7 Folder 28
Oration of Honorable O.P. Morton, address of Major General George G. Meade and dedication ode for National Cemetery at Gettysburg
 1869-07-01
Box 7
Printed notices relating to Faust
 Date unknown
Box 7
Appendix to editorial notes for Bayard's poems prepared by Mrs. Bayard Taylor
 1895
Box 7
Biographical sketch for the intended Cambridge edition
 1895-1896
Box 7
A few letters to Bayard Taylor
 1877-1878
Box 7
Small autograph-type album with poems and flower pictures
 Date unknown
Box 12 Folder 54
Scrapbook I
 1847-1876
Scope and Contents
News clippings
Box 12 Folder 55
Scrapbook II
 1877-1881
Scope and Contents
News clippings, some manuscripts
Box 10 Folder 56
Scrapbook III
 1878
Scope and Contents
Clippings, correspondence, and printed items relating to farewell dinner for B.T.
Box 10 Folder 57
Scrapbook IIIa
 1876-1878
Scope and Contents
Clippings, regrets about not being able to attend farewell dinner for B.T. (1878-04-04), printed items, 1876 Centennial Exposition at Philadelphia, PA.
Box 10 Folder 58
Scrapbook
 1878-1879
Scope and Contents
Clippings, correspondence, and printed items regarding Bayard Taylors death.
Box 10 Folder 59
Scrapbook V
 1860-1876
Scope and Contents
Bayard Taylor's contributions to newspapers. Does not contain correspondence, but does include some loose newspapers.
Box 10 Folder 60
Scrapbook VI
 1876-1878
Scope and Contents
Bayard Taylor's contributions to newspapers and magazines.
Box 11 Folder 8
Manuscripts
 1895-1896
Scope and Contents
The package, containing manuscripts on yellow paper, is labelled "The within edition of Bayard Taylor's Completed Poems was prepared by me [presumably Mrs. Taylor] in the years 1895-1896 in the hopes that Houghton's, Mifflin & Co., would publish it. They, however, declined to do so."
Box 11 Folder 22
Miscellaneous printed matter, in German and English
 Date unknown
Box 11 Folder 22
Printed page proofs of the play "Prince Deukalion"
 Date unknown
Box 11 Folder 22
Five manuscript and typed pages of autobiographical sketch by Bayard Taylor
 Date unknown
Box 12 Folder 31
Blank Book III
 1824-1828
Scope and Contents
First few pages appear to have postage accounts of John Taylor. Pages also have childish scribblings and drawings. Also contains some written essays and poems.
Box 12 Folder 40
Diary inscribed J.B. Taylor
 1842-01-1842-04-01
Scope and Contents
Shelved on K-136-A-4-A
Box 12 Folder 33
Wallet type folder containing miscellaneous writings, poems, etc.
 Date Unknown
Box 12 Folder 33
Blank Book V
 1840-1843
Box 12 Folder 11
Manuscripts of early prose writings - scraps
 Undated
Box 12 Folder 9
Manuscript poems, etc.
 1837-1878
Scope and Contents
In binder.
Box 12 Folder 21
The National Ode manuscripts
 1876-07-04
Scope and Contents
This consists of 9 pages in B.T.'s handwriting submitted in 1876 for reproduction by facsimile to Jas. R. Osgood & Co., Boston.
Series II. Journals and Notebooks
Scope and Contents
Diaries were numbered in no particular order. The numbering often continues where the folder numbers leave off for that box, and were not numbered separately from folders.
Box 9 Volume 29
Schooldays - notes and sketches
 1837-1838
Box 9 Volume 41
Diary
 1844
Scope and Contents
Diary for January and February followed by sparse entries for remainder of the year: Arrived in England July, arrived in Heidelburg September, arrived in Frankfurt October.
Box 9 Volume 42
Sketch of a ramble through Europe
 1844-1845
Scope and Contents
Begun on the Atlantic, July 5, 1844. Finished in Florence, December 19, 1845.
Box 9 Volume 30
Original poems
 1845
Box 9 Volume 32
Notebook of places visited in Germany, with poems and expenditures
 1845
Box 9 Volume 43
Diary
 1845-12-22-1846-06
Scope and Contents
Italy, France, and England.
Box 9 Volume 35
First drafts of poems
 1845-05-10
Box 9 Volume 44
Diary
 1847-01-21-1854
Scope and Contents
Begins in Phoenixville, January 21, 1847. Intermittent entries to December 1950, and one entry for June 10, 1854. Included personal thoughts, prayers, and self-analysis.
Box 9 Volume 45
Diary
 1849-11-06-1950-01-22
Scope and Contents
Intermittent entries, with some expenses and miscellaneous notes. United States and Mexico.
Box 9 Volume 34
Pencil sketches of various places in Mexico, and poems from 1850
 1849-1850
Box 9 Volume 36
Diary
 Undated
Scope and Contents
Travel descriptions, including Albania, Corfu, etc. Also poems, expenditures, and miscellaneous information (undated)
Box 9 Volume 46
Diary
 1851-09-19-1852-02
Scope and Contents
Begins in London, followed by Germany (October 5, 1851), ends with Ethiopia.
Box 9 Volume 47
Diary
 1852-02-09-1852-04-01
Scope and Contents
Begins in Ethiopia, ends in Cairo.
Box 9 Volume 48
Diary
 1852-04-22-1852-07-13
Scope and Contents
Begins in Syria, ends in Greece.
Box 9 Volume 37
Small notebook containing original poems and some sketches
 1853
Box 9 Volume 49
Diary
 1852-11-1853-12
Scope and Contents
Begins in Gibraltar, ends in India, Singapore, and China.
Box 8 Volume 50
Journal
 1853
Scope and Contents
China with some sketches
Box 8 Volume 51
Diary
 1856-12-1857-01
Scope and Contents
Scandinavia.
Box 8 Volume 52
Diary
 1857-1858
Scope and Contents
Norway, Greece, and Poland. Apologies at the end of the diary that "journals for the past seven years have not been more complete."
Box 8 Volume 53
Diary
 1857-07-1857-09
Scope and Contents
Scandinavia.
Box 8 Volume 38
Diary pages
 1866-06-29-1866-07-02
Scope and Contents
United States.
Box 8 Volume 39
Diary
 1867-04, 1867-02
Scope and Contents
Trip to Nuremburg, Munich, Majorca, and Spain, April to June 1867. Also a trip to Italy, February 1868.
Box 8 Volume 62
Notes from diaries and Journals I
 1842-1858
Scope and Contents
Apparently kept by Mrs. Taylor.
Box 8 Volume 63
Notes from diaries and Journals II
 1858-1868
Scope and Contents
Apparently kept by Mrs. Taylor.
Box 8 Volume 64
Notes from diaries and Journals III
 1868-1878
Scope and Contents
Apparently kept by Mrs. Taylor.
Box 8 Folder 56
Letters, miscellaneous catalogue No. I
 Date unknown
Box 8 Folder 61
Reviews of the translation of Faust
 Date unknown
Box 8 Folder 65
Notes
 1837
Scope and Contents
Notes of books read or bought, translations made, names of poems written by Bayard Taylor.
Box 8 Folder 67
Clippings and articles
 Date unknown
Scope and Contents
Printed and written, some by Bayard Taylor.
Series III. Incoming correspondence
Scope and Contents
All letters are originals unless otherwise noted.
